english: Indonesian Sweet Pancake Cheese Condensed Milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for martabak manis; 'pancake food product' is the closest defensible parent class. Exact match unavailable.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes and similar products) used as closest defensible category for martabak manis; no exact FoodEx2 entry for Indonesian sweet stuffed pancake.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for martabak manis or Indonesian sweet pancake; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 10 pieces (1 whole martabak manis, approximately 24 cm diameter)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is the full pancake of 10 pieces (approximately 500 g), though a typical individual portion is 3 pieces (approximately 150 g).

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe (SR Legacy ID 18259) and Cheese, cheddar (SR Legacy ID 01009), adapted for martabak manis cheesy milk composition
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo entry exists for martabak manis cheesy milk. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for plain pancake (FDC ID 18259: energy 227 kcal, protein 6.36 g, fat 9.67 g, carbohydrate 28.3 g per 100 g) and cheddar cheese (FDC ID 01009: energy 403 kcal, protein 24.9 g, fat 33.1 g, saturated fat 21.1 g, calcium 721 mg per 100 g), weighted to reflect the thick Indonesian-style batter enriched with additional butter, eggs, sugar, and condensed milk, and stuffed with cheddar cheese. The resulting composite yields values consistent with the grabfood input (energy 320 kcal, protein 8 g, fat 18.67 g, carbohydrate 30 g, sugar 18.67 g, sodium 233.33 mg, fibre 0.67 g, calcium 133.33 mg, iron 1.0 mg, cholesterol 73.33 mg, saturated fat 10.0 g, trans fat 0.33 g per 100 g), which are retained with medium confidence. Singapore HPB Foodo database (https://foodo.sg/) was consulted but no direct martabak manis cheesy milk entry was found as of 2026-04-17. The grabfood menu listing URL (https://foodo.sg/) cited in the input record supports only menu_listing provenance and is not used as a nutrient authority.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pancakes, made from mix, GI 67 (glucose reference = 100)
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal); University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak manis cheesy milk in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. GI 67 is taken from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:12) for pancakes made from mix (glucose reference = 100), which is the closest defensible analogous food available in an approved GI source. Martabak manis uses a similar refined wheat flour batter with added sugar and dairy enrichment; the thick batter and cheese filling may marginally lower the effective GI relative to plain pancake mix, but GI 67 is retained as a conservative estimate. Glycaemic load per 150 g individual serving (3 pieces) calculated as: (67 × 30.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.5) / 100 = 30.15, rounded to 30.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (18.67 g/100 g) with low fibre (0.67 g/100 g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium 233 mg/100 g adds direct vascular load; saturated fat 10 g/100 g contributes to endothelial dysfunction.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit martabak manis cheesy milk due to its high sugar content (18.67 g/100 g) exceeding the 10 g/100 g threshold with fibre below 2 g/100 g, which independently elevates blood pressure risk via insulin-driven sodium retention (AHA 2018), compounded by 233 mg/100 g sodium and 10 g/100 g saturated fat.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 18.67 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g hard gate with dietary fibre only 0.67 g/100 g; GI 67 (medium) with high carbohydrate density (30 g/100 g) produces a high glycaemic load of 30.2 per 150 g serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Martabak manis cheesy milk is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; the combination of 18.67 g/100 g sugar, 30 g/100 g total carbohydrate, negligible fibre (0.67 g/100 g), and a glycaemic load of 30.2 per 150 g serving creates a rapid and sustained postprandial glucose spike that is incompatible with glycaemic management targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 10 g/100 g (53% of total fat) from butter, cheddar cheese, and condensed milk raises LDL cholesterol; trans fat 0.33 g/100 g from butter or margarine further elevates cardiovascular risk; cholesterol 73.33 mg/100 g adds dietary cholesterol load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid martabak manis cheesy milk; saturated fat at 10 g/100 g substantially exceeds the 5 g/100 g CVD threshold, trans fat at 0.33 g/100 g is present, and the combination of high sugar, saturated fat, and dietary cholesterol (73.33 mg/100 g) creates a compounded atherogenic risk profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 10 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g RA pro-inflammatory threshold; refined sugar 18.67 g/100 g promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine production; trans fat 0.33 g/100 g is a hard RA disqualifier.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Martabak manis cheesy milk is unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to saturated fat at 10 g/100 g, trans fat at 0.33 g/100 g, and high refined sugar at 18.67 g/100 g, all of which promote systemic inflammation and exacerbate RA disease activity.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak Manis Cheesy Milk (10 pieces) is an Indonesian-style thick sweet stuffed pancake (martabak manis, also known as terang bulan) filled with cheddar cheese and drizzled with sweetened condensed milk, served as a full pancake of 10 slices weighing approximately 500 g. The batter is made from refined wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, yeast, and baking powder, producing a thick, spongy, honeycomb-textured interior with a crisp, buttery outer crust. The filling of melted cheddar cheese and condensed milk creates a rich, sweet-savoury flavour profile. Martabak manis originated in Indonesia and is widely sold as a street food and dessert snack across Singapore and Southeast Asia. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 320 kcal, 8 g protein, 18.67 g fat (of which 10 g is saturated fat and 0.33 g is trans fat), 30 g carbohydrate, 18.67 g sugar, 0.67 g dietary fibre, 233 mg sodium, 133 mg calcium, and 73 mg cholesterol. The high sugar content (18.67 g/100 g), high saturated fat (10 g/100 g), and low fibre (0.67 g/100 g) make this food clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at 67 (medium) based on analogous pancake data from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), with a glycaemic load of 30.2 per 150 g individual serving. It is halal and vegetarian but contains gluten, dairy, and eggs.
common_user_queries: Is martabak manis suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for martabak manis cheesy milk exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for plain pancake (FDC ID 18259) and cheddar cheese (FDC ID 01009), weighted to reflect the enriched Indonesian-style batter and cheese filling. The adapted composite is consistent with the grabfood input values (confidence 0.7). Medium confidence is assigned because the adaptation is compositionally defensible but not directly verified against a laboratory analysis of this specific commercial product.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin profile, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific commercial martabak manis cheesy milk formulation are not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC IDs 18259, 01009) or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-17. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: martabak manis is a cooked wheat-flour-and-dairy product with no vitamin C-contributing ingredients; USDA SR Legacy pancake entry (FDC ID 18259) reports 0 mg vitamin C, confirmed negligible. The input source URL https://foodo.sg/ cited in the original record supports only menu_listing provenance and is not used as a nutrient authority; nutrient values were re-derived from USDA SR Legacy as the approved primary source. Trans fat 0.33 g/100 g is retained from input; source is likely butter or margarine used in commercial preparation, consistent with USDA SR Legacy butter entries. Glycaemic load per serving calculated for a 150 g individual portion (3 pieces): (67 × 30.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 1.5) / 100 = 30.15, rounded to 30.2. The food is classified as Indonesian cuisine with country_of_origin Singapore, reflecting that this specific commercial product is sold in Singapore by a grabfood vendor using an Indonesian-origin recipe. The input health_suitability references Biscoff spread as an ingredient; this is not confirmed in the canonical cheesy milk original description and has been excluded from the primary ingredient list.
